thai_meaning: ยอมแพ้, เลิกล้ม
eng_definition: to stop trying to do something
example_sentence: Don't give up — you are almost there.

thai_meaning: ดูแล, เอาใจใส่
eng_definition: to take care of someone or something
example_sentence: She looks after her little brother after school.

thai_meaning: ค้นพบ, รู้ความจริง
eng_definition: to discover information about something
example_sentence: I need to find out when the train leaves.

thai_meaning: เลื่อนออกไป, ผัดผ่อน
eng_definition: to delay or postpone something
example_sentence: They put off the meeting until Friday.

thai_meaning: ดำเนินการ, ทำให้สำเร็จ
eng_definition: to perform or complete a task
example_sentence: The scientists carried out the experiment carefully.

thai_meaning: คิดออก, เสนอ (ความคิด)
eng_definition: to think of an idea or plan
example_sentence: She came up with a clever solution to the problem.

thai_meaning: ใช้จนหมด, ไม่มีเหลือ
eng_definition: to have no more of something left
example_sentence: We ran out of milk this morning.

thai_meaning: ปฏิเสธ (ข้อเสนอ)
eng_definition: to refuse an offer or request
example_sentence: He turned down the job offer in Bangkok.

thai_meaning: หยิบยก (เรื่อง) ขึ้นมาพูด; เลี้ยงดู
eng_definition: to mention a topic, or to raise a child
example_sentence: She brought up an interesting point during the discussion.

thai_meaning: เข้ากันได้ดี
eng_definition: to have a friendly relationship with someone
example_sentence: The two new students get along very well.

thai_meaning: นาน ๆ ครั้ง, แทบจะไม่เคย
eng_definition: very rarely
example_sentence: We eat at expensive restaurants once in a blue moon.

thai_meaning: ตั้งใจอ่านหนังสือ (อย่างหนัก)
eng_definition: to study hard
example_sentence: Exams are next week, so I need to hit the books.

thai_meaning: ง่ายมาก, เรื่องกล้วย ๆ
eng_definition: something very easy to do
example_sentence: The grammar test was a piece of cake.

thai_meaning: รู้สึกไม่ค่อยสบาย
eng_definition: feeling slightly ill
example_sentence: She stayed home because she felt under the weather.

thai_meaning: ทำลายความเงียบ, เริ่มต้นบทสนทนาให้ผ่อนคลาย
eng_definition: to make people feel more relaxed and start talking
example_sentence: He told a joke to break the ice at the meeting.

thai_meaning: เข้าใจตรงกัน, คิดเห็นไปในทางเดียวกัน
eng_definition: in agreement or sharing the same understanding
example_sentence: Before we start, let's make sure we're on the same page.

word: cost an arm and a leg
thai_meaning: แพงมาก
eng_definition: to be very expensive
example_sentence: That new smartphone costs an arm and a leg.

thai_meaning: เกิดอาการกลัวหรือลังเลในนาทีสุดท้าย
eng_definition: to suddenly become too nervous to do something planned
example_sentence: He got cold feet just before giving his speech.

thai_meaning: ฟางเส้นสุดท้าย, จุดที่ทนไม่ไหวอีกต่อไป
eng_definition: the final problem that makes someone lose patience
example_sentence: Being late again was the last straw for the manager.

thai_meaning: คอยเฝ้าดู, คอยระวัง
eng_definition: to watch someone or something carefully
example_sentence: Could you keep an eye on my bag for a moment?
